steps.title: Define variables and write the equations
steps.content:
Let b = price of one loaf of bread (in dollars) and e = price of one dozen eggs (in dollars).
The first customer’s purchase gives:
$$ b + 2e = 12.45 $$
The second customer’s purchase gives:
$$ 4b + e = 19.42 $$

steps.title: Eliminate b to solve for e
steps.content:
Multiply the first equation by 4 to align the bread terms:
$$ 4b + 8e = 49.80 $$
Subtract the second equation from this result:
$$ (4b + 8e) - (4b + e) = 49.80 - 19.42 $$
$$ 7e = 30.38 $$
Divide both sides by 7:
$$ e = \frac{30.38}{7} = 4.34 $$
This value matches choice D — D is correct.
